element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational combat mechanics taught against entry-level opponent
Player learns stamina depletes from missed punches; must manage energy between rounds via button mashing

element_introduction_order.element: Special attacks (spin hook, rope push, uppercut)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Timing and distance-based activation; high risk/reward (one-hit KO potential or heavy counter damage)
Advanced defensive technique requiring pattern recognition of opponent behavior

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Between matches via power point allocation
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Long-term progression system; player tailors boxer build to upcoming opponent types

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Post-championship in arcade; recurring in NES
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Perpetual loop against strongest three opponents (Blue Warker, Green Hante, Onetta Yank); mastery required for sustained play

typical_relief_triggers: Round interval (stamina recovery phase via button mashing)
typical_tension_triggers: Counter-punch risk window during special attack startup
Progressive opponent difficulty increase (escalating boxer strength)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based tension and release within each round; matches build toward climactic moments when special attacks are available. Difficulty escalation creates sustained tension across career progression.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Entry-level opponent (Violence Jo) functions as tutorial fighter; basic mechanics only
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating opponent variety and complexity; counter-punch patterns become unpredictable; special attack timing windows tighten
difficulty_curve.late_game: Championship defense against three champion-tier boxers (Blue Warker, Green Hante, Onetta Yank) with advanced AI and devastating special attacks
notable_spikes: First championship match (transition from progression to defense mode)
Encounters with Blue Warker, Green Hante, Onetta Yank (peak difficulty tier)

notable_level_patterns.description: Eight sequential opponents of increasing difficulty; each win advances player toward world championship. NES version: Violence Jo > Brown Pants > White Wolf > Bomba Vern > Beat Brown > Blue Warker > Green Hante > Onetta Yank. Arcade version differs slightly in champion designation.
Post-championship, arcade version cycles only through final three opponents (Blue Warker, Green Hante, Onetta Yank); NES version supports password saves for extended play beyond championship
Between-round progression system; player allocates earned power points to punch, stamina, or speed attributes, enabling adaptive boxer development and meta-level strategy

sources_used: tcrf - Ring King (NES) from The Cutting Room Floor
wikipedia - Ring King gameplay mechanics and opponent roster
